columns.extraction_plan: The control arm is referred to as "SOC-alone" or "SOC" (Standard-of-Care alone). This is explicitly stated in the abstract and methods section. From the abstract (Chunk 5, page 3, "CANCER THERAPY AND PREVENTION" section): "The comparison randomised patients 1:1 to SOC-alone with or without daily abiraterone acetate 1000 mg + prednisolone 5 mg (SOC + AAP), continued until disease progression." From the "Randomisation and masking" section (Chunk 6, page 4): "Allocation was 1:1 to standard-of-care (SOC-alone) only group or SOC-alone with abiraterone acetate and prednisolone/prednisone group (SOC + AAP)." The tables (Table 1 on page 5, Table 2 on page 6, Table 3 on page 8) and figures (Figure 2 on page 7, Figure 3 on page 9) also use "SOC-alone" or "SOC" to denote the control group.
